# Kondaveedu Fort

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Dec 29, 2021

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

1

Share

Kondaveedu Fort is one of the most formidable hill forts of Andhra Pradesh, often spoken of in the same breath as Bobbili when it comes to courage and valor.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!yphv!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4a99ba5e-9565-4a0d-9ad9-54c15bb10ac8_259x194.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!yphv!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F4a99ba5e-9565-4a0d-9ad9-54c15bb10ac8_259x194.png "Image")

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!zR3z!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F67dba8e2-61bc-44f2-98a3-f3cc6d5256d0_259x194.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!zR3z!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F67dba8e2-61bc-44f2-98a3-f3cc6d5256d0_259x194.png "Image")

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Ozks!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fac2e000f-c611-4220-8251-2306dc0655a9_284x177.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Ozks!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fac2e000f-c611-4220-8251-2306dc0655a9_284x177.png "Image")

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!OzE0!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F9d44e929-b398-416b-b88f-d17cb233a351_275x183.png)

![Image](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!OzE0!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F9d44e929-b398-416b-b88f-d17cb233a351_275x183.png "Image")

**History**

It was first established as a settlement in 1115 CE by Gopanna,the commander of Buddhavarma of Telugu Chola dynasty. Later absorbed into the Kakatiya realm, becoming part of their network of strongholds.

After the fall of the Kakatiyas, Andhra was absorbed into the Delhi Sultanate under the Tughlaqs in 1323 CE. A confederation of Hindu chieftains, led by the Musunuri Nayaks, rose against the Tughlaq governor. Their alliance restored native rule in much of coastal Andhra.

Among these allies was *Prolaya Vema Reddy*, who established the Reddy dynasty. Initially feudatories of the Kakatiyas, the Reddys carved out their own independent kingdom in the Krishna–Guntur region. Vema Reddy moved his capital from Addanki to Kondaveedu, transforming the hill fort into a seat of power and a symbol of Telugu pride.

The fort eventually fell under the Bahmani Sultans and later the Gajapati rulers of Odisha, continuing its role as a contested stronghold in Deccan politics.

In 1513 Sri Krishnadeva Raya, in his campaign against the Gajapatis of Odisha, laid a long and bloody siege to Kondaveedu. Prince Virabhadra led a fierce defense, embodying the fort’s reputation for valor. The breakthrough came when Timmarasu, Raya’s trusted minister, discovered a secret entrance. In a daring night assault, Kondaveedu fell to Vijayanagara.

Following the decline of Vijayanagara, Kondaveedu passed into the hands of the Golconda-based Qutub Shahis. Later, the fort was absorbed into the Mughal empire and subsequently the Hyderabad-based Asaf Jahi dynasty. Finally, the British seized Kondaveedu in 1788, marking the end of its centuries-long role as a contested bastion.

**The Fort Complex**

The fort sits on a range of hills at 1,500 feet, with its highest point reaching 1,700 feet above sea level. A steep but short climb,challenging, direct, and suited for those seeking intensity. A gentler 3.2 km ascent, less tiring, allowing visitors to soak in the landscape and atmosphere.These natural defenses made Kondaveedu one of the toughest forts to besiege, amplifying its reputation for resilience.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ERIg!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F0f665993-6566-4f4a-87bb-d629574aad6d_240x128.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ERIg!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F0f665993-6566-4f4a-87bb-d629574aad6d_240x128.jpeg)

Kondaveedu is not just one fort but a **complex of three hill forts**, each layered with history. The oldest fort dates back to the 12th century Telugu Chola period, representing the earliest settlement on the hill. The main fort dating to the 14th century was built by the Reddy rulers when Prolaya Vema Reddy shifted his capital here, located at an elevation of 1050 ft.

Considered the strongest of the three, with **about 21 structures inside,**including bastions, gateways, temples, and water systems, it served as administrative and military hub of the Reddy dynasty. Additional fortifications were reinforced by later dynasties of Gajapatis, Vijayanagara, Bahmani and Qutub Shah. Together, the three forts formed a defensive triad across the Kondaveedu hills.

There are 2 entrance gates leading into the fort, the Kollepalli Darwaza built with **massive granite stone blocks**, rising three stories high. Its imposing scale symbolized authority and deterrence. The other gateway was Nadella Darwaza, also built with granite, rising three stories, serving as a defensive choke point, controlling access to the fort’s interior.

Among other structures is a building constructed with **rock slabs**, adding to the fort’s rugged yet enduring character. Within the main fort, about **21 structures** survive. bastions, temples, water reservoirs, and administrative spaces.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!1rAC!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F5673a3f4-c5da-4b4d-b4d3-8d18208ef8ef_300x168.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!1rAC!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F5673a3f4-c5da-4b4d-b4d3-8d18208ef8ef_300x168.jpeg)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Ntju!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F46796da1-265d-43a9-9219-710dc0b33d93_299x168.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Ntju!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F46796da1-265d-43a9-9219-710dc0b33d93_299x168.jpeg)

The fort has 3 reservoirs, Mutyalamma Cheruvu, named after the Grama Devata Mutyalamma, served as one of the primary water sources for the fort’s residents. Putalamma Cheruvu, dedicated to another Grama Devata, whose embankments ensured year-round storage, even during dry seasons. The third one Vedulla Cheruvu, sustained life inside the fort during sieges and long occupations, completing the water system.

The reservoirs were built by utilizing **natural depressions and rock cavities**, reinforced with embankments.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!N-Ds!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F72cd8e0a-8f95-4e0c-93b8-1233f03102f7_240x134.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!N-Ds!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F72cd8e0a-8f95-4e0c-93b8-1233f03102f7_240x134.jpeg)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Lr6D!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F742fee6e-d2df-4d1a-a995-2a1a50bece3d_355x142.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Lr6D!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F742fee6e-d2df-4d1a-a995-2a1a50bece3d_355x142.jpeg)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!xrN3!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F29393895-d0e2-4e73-ae3d-3e3008d7211f_259x194.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!xrN3!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F29393895-d0e2-4e73-ae3d-3e3008d7211f_259x194.jpeg)

The **Katthula Bavi (Well of Knives)** is one of Kondaveedu’s most chilling and intriguing attractions, adding a darker layer to its legacy. Situated within the *Gopinatha Swamy temple* at the foothills of Kondaveedu, it was used to dispose of rivals and enemies,those who were killed were thrown into this well.

Locals describe it as a trap-like structure, sometimes referred to as*“Cheekati Koneru” (Dark Pond)*. Its construction was meant to deceive and eliminate foes. The site carries a grim reputation, remembered as a place where betrayal and execution were enacted under the guise of sacred space.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!vVAN!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F3a2e6a08-5835-473b-ad9e-dfb9eb10e4e4_318x159.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!vVAN!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F3a2e6a08-5835-473b-ad9e-dfb9eb10e4e4_318x159.jpeg)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!AmCq!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fc1d0df44-4770-4c2b-a33c-f59ca224345d_240x187.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!AmCq!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fc1d0df44-4770-4c2b-a33c-f59ca224345d_240x187.jpeg)

Currently a ghat road has been built for the benefit of visitors to Kondaveedu, and is quite a scenic drive all the way up with some crazy twists and turns, be warned though you can get caught up in some traffic jams on weekends here.
